text.title: Use Case Modeling — Five-Step Process
text.summary: Step-by-step process for producing a UC catalog: identify actors, identify UCs, create UC diagram, write UC specs, validate.

rule.statement: Lock the system boundary in writing before identifying actors. Every actor identification prompt must include that boundary paragraph verbatim.
rule.rationale: Without a written boundary, actors expand to include every person who touches any related system, producing an unmanageable diagram.

list.item: What external systems connect to this system?
Who maintains the system or receives its outputs?
What time-based or event-based triggers initiate system behavior?

rule.statement: Name every use case as Verb + Noun from the actor's perspective. Reject names that do not pass this test.
rule.rationale: Verb + Noun names (Place Order, Generate Report, Manage User Account) encode the actor's goal; feature names (Order System, Report Module) encode the system's structure.

list.item: What tasks does this actor perform with the system?
What information does the actor need from the system?
What does the actor create, update, or delete?
What events trigger the actor's actions?

rule.statement: Cap the UC count per release at approximately 30. UC sets larger than 30 collapse into documents nobody re-reads.
rule.rationale: Useful UC count is bounded by stakeholder review capacity; beyond 30 per release, the diagram becomes an archive rather than a working tool.

section.title: Step 3: Create UC Diagram
section.p: The UC diagram visualizes actors and UCs within the system boundary. Relationships (include/extend/generalization) are added only after all UCs are drafted and reviewed.

rule.statement: Run the relationship pass (include/extend/generalization) last, using only Opus or equivalent, only after all UCs are drafted, and require a one-line rationale per edge.
rule.rationale: Relationship inference is the highest-error part of UC modeling; drafters and reviewers both default to incorrect extend directionality. The relationship pass requires global view of the full UC set.

section.reference: Script emitting a PlantUML UC diagram from a use-case-index.json
UC diagram template listing actors, UCs per actor, and relationships

rule.statement: Each UC spec must have all nine elements: name/ID, description, actors, preconditions, main flow (5-9 steps), alternative flows (at least 1), exception flows (at least 1), postconditions, business rules.
rule.rationale: Missing elements — especially exception flows — produce specs that pass review and fail in production. Postconditions must be observable (DB row, message, file, audit log).

rule.statement: Store UCs as markdown with YAML frontmatter containing id, name, primary_actor, status, version, regulation_anchors, and source. Persist under .aidocs/<feature>/use-cases/UC-NNN-<slug>.md.
rule.rationale: Frontmatter feeds ReqIF export, the index script, and CI checks. Source citations prevent hallucinated requirements.

rule.statement: Use an adversarially framed reviewer prompt (different model or adversarial system prompt) — not the same model that drafted the UC — to score each UC against the 9-item quality checklist.
rule.rationale: Drafter and reviewer using the same model and prompt yields self-congratulatory reviews; adversarial framing surfaces gaps the drafter skipped.

list.item: Are all actor goals covered by at least one UC?
Is the main flow correct from the actor's perspective?
Are exception flows handled with observable postconditions?
